back_translation: Now Jericho was tightly shut up because [they] feared the people of Israel; no one was going out and no one was coming in.
The LORD said to Joshua, 'Behold, I have given Jericho along with its king and its valiant warriors into your hand.'
You shall march around the city — that is, all the soldiers — to march around the city one time per day. Do this for six days.
Have seven priests carry seven rams' horns and walk in front of the ark; and on the seventh day, you shall march around the city seven times while the priests blow the horns.

And when there is a long blast on the ram's horn, when you hear the sound of the trumpet, have all the soldiers shout a loud shout. Then the wall of the city will collapse, and the soldiers will charge up — each one straight into the section in front of him.
So Joshua son of Nun called the priests and said to them, 'Take up the ark of the covenant, and have seven priests carry seven rams' horns and walk in front of the ark of the LORD.'
Then he told the people, 'Advance and march around the city; let the armed troops move ahead of the ark of the LORD.'

When Joshua finished speaking to the people, the seven priests carrying seven rams' horns before the presence of the LORD advanced and blew the horns, and the ark of the covenant of the LORD followed behind them.
The armed troops were walking in front of the priests who were blowing the horns, and the rear guard followed behind the ark, while the sound of the horns continued unceasingly.
But Joshua had commanded the soldiers: 'You shall not shout, do not let your voice be heard, and let no word come out of your mouths until the day I tell you, "Shout!" Then you shall shout.'
So Joshua had the ark of the LORD move around the city, marching around the city one time. Then they returned to the camp and spent the night in the camp.
Joshua got up early in the morning, and the priests took up the ark of the LORD.

The seven priests carrying seven rams' horns in front of the ark of the LORD walked on and blew the horns without stopping; the armed troops were in front of them, and the rear guard followed the ark of the LORD, while the sound of the horns continued unceasingly.
They marched around the city on the second day one time, then returned to the camp. They did this throughout six days.
On the seventh day, they got up early from dawn and marched around the city in the same manner seven times. Only on that day did they march around the city seven times.
At the seventh time around, when the priests blew the horns, Joshua commanded the soldiers, 'Shout! For the LORD has given this city to you.'
This city and everything in it shall be devoted to the LORD for total destruction. Only Rahab the prostitute and everyone with her in the house shall survive, because she hid the spies whom we sent.

But you, keep yourselves away from the things devoted to destruction, lest you yourselves become those devoted to destruction. If you take any of the devoted things, you will make the camp of Israel devoted to destruction and bring disaster upon that camp.
All the silver, gold, vessels of bronze and iron are holy to the LORD; they must be brought into the treasury of the LORD.

Then the soldiers shouted, and the priests blew the horns. When the soldiers heard the sound of the horn, they shouted a loud shout, and the wall collapsed. Then the soldiers charged up into the city — each one straight into the section in front of him — and they captured the city.
They completely destroyed everything in the city with the edge of the sword — both man and woman, young people and old people, including ox, sheep, and donkey.
As for the two men who had spied out the land, Joshua commanded them, 'Go into the house of that prostitute woman and bring out the woman and all who belong to her, just as you swore to her.'

The young men who had been spies went in and brought out Rahab, her father, mother, brothers, and all who belonged to her. They brought out all the members of her family and settled them outside the camp of Israel.
They burned the city and everything in it with fire. Only the silver, gold, vessels of bronze and iron did they place in the treasury of the house of the LORD.

Joshua spared Rahab the prostitute, the household of her father, and all who belonged to her, because she had hidden the spies whom Joshua had sent to scout out Jericho. And she has lived among Israel to this day.

At that time Joshua proclaimed a solemn oath: 'May whoever rises up and rebuilds this city of Jericho be cursed before the LORD. He shall lay the foundation of the city at the cost of his firstborn son, and he shall set up the gates of the city at the cost of his youngest son.'
And the LORD was with Joshua, and his fame spread throughout the land.
